close_reason: Kept .to_string() since split returns Iter[StringView]; changed char_length() to length() on the converted String.
description: nav.mbt:13 - split('/') yields Iter[StringView]. Check if .to_string() is needed; if segments are already String, remove the no-op copy.
title: Simplify: parse_path calls .to_string() on split segments

close_reason: Merged boot() into start(), eliminating the after_update callback indirection.
description: app.mbt - boot is package-private with exactly one caller (start). The callback indirection adds a closure allocation with no modularity benefit. Consider inlining or merging.
title: Arch: boot takes after_update callback unnecessarily

acceptance_criteria:
- Coverage matrix defined for core modules
- New unit test suites added and passing in CI
- Regression-prone runtime paths explicitly covered
- Developer docs include how to run unit tests locally
close_reason: all steps complete
description:
Establish a comprehensive, layered unit testing strategy for the Chai framework beyond current example-driven Playwright coverage.

Scope:
- Define module-level coverage targets
- Add fast unit test harnesses (pure logic + DOM-focused runtime behavior)
- Add CI quality gates and coverage reporting

Primary goals:
- Catch regressions in core runtime logic before browser-level tests
- Keep tests fast and deterministic
- Make diff/reconciliation behavior directly testable
title: Build comprehensive unit testing for Chai runtime

acceptance_criteria: A committed coverage matrix exists with module->test-suite mapping, risk ranking, and explicit regression links.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive unit-testing rollout (white-box suites, regression coverage, CI unit workflow, docs/scripts).
description:
Produce a concrete unit-testing architecture document for this package.

Scope:
- Inventory exported APIs and internal high-risk runtime paths (cmd/sub/nav/diff/app/component/attr/vnode)
- Define test layers: pure unit tests, DOM-behavior unit tests, and minimal end-to-end smoke tests
- Decide test-file layout (`*_test.mbt`) and naming/filter strategy
- Define a coverage matrix tied to known regressions and recent bug IDs

Output is planning + structure only, no broad refactors.
title: Define unit-test architecture and coverage matrix

acceptance_criteria: A shared harness exists and moon test can execute deterministic unit tests without Playwright.

Build reusable test harness primitives required by unit suites.

Scope:
- Add MoonBit test scaffolding for this package (fast local execution via `moon test`)
- Introduce seam helpers/mocks for JS/webapi interactions used by Cmd/Sub/Nav/App/Diff paths
- Add fixtures for deterministic dispatch capture and scheduler/timer behavior
- Ensure harness supports both pure logic tests and DOM-manipulation assertions

This issue is infrastructure only; behavior suites land in follow-up tasks.
title: Add MoonBit unit-test harness and webapi test seams

acceptance_criteria: Core pure/runtime constructors and mappers have exhaustive deterministic tests including nullish HTTP rejection fallback behavior.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive unit-testing rollout (white-box suites, regression coverage, CI unit workflow, docs/scripts).
description:
Add comprehensive pure/unit suites for deterministic logic not requiring DOM tree mutation.

Target APIs:
- Cmd: none/batch/map/task/send/after/http_get error extraction fallbacks
- Sub: none/batch/map/sub composition semantics
- Attr + VNode: map transformations and helper constructors
- Nav parsing helpers: path/query/hash parsing behavior across edge cases

Favor small focused tests and regression pinning for nullish error reasons in HTTP failures.
title: Add pure logic unit suites for Cmd/Sub/Attr/VNode/Nav parsing

acceptance_criteria: Diff unit suites cover keyed/unkeyed edge cases and detect stale or corrupted DOM regressions before e2e tests.
close_reason: Implemented comprehensive unit-testing rollout (white-box suites, regression coverage, CI unit workflow, docs/scripts).
description:
Add DOM-focused unit tests for reconciliation behavior in `diff.mbt`.

Scope:
- Attribute/property/event/style patching and removals
- Child diff behavior for append/remove/replace flows
- Keyed reconciliation correctness (reorder/move/insert/remove)
- Failure behavior for mixed keyed/non-keyed siblings and duplicate keys
- Mode-switch replacement behavior (keyed <-> non-keyed) and stale-node prevention

Tests should directly assert DOM state transitions via harness fixtures.
title: Add DOM reconciliation unit tests for diff engine

acceptance_criteria: Component unit tests validate lifecycle, teardown, pending init commands, and disconnected-handle semantics.

Add component lifecycle/unit tests for isolated component runtime semantics.

Scope:

- Handle wiring, `Cmd::send`, and disconnected-handle warning-once behavior

- Slot cleanup/teardown when components disappear across renders

Include regression tests for unmounted handle dispatch behavior.

acceptance_criteria: Boot/update/subscription orchestration has deterministic ordering tests and cleanup guarantees.

Use lightweight harness spies to assert call ordering and cleanup guarantees.

close_reason: Added duplicate-key guards for old/new keyed children in reconciliation
description:
`diff_children_keyed` uses `Map[String, (Int, VNode)]`, so duplicate keys overwrite previous entries. This can leave old duplicate-key nodes untracked and never removed, resulting in stale DOM nodes and nondeterministic updates.

Fix direction: validate uniqueness of keys in both old and new keyed lists and abort on duplicates with a precise message (`duplicate key: ...`).
title: Keyed reconciliation silently corrupts DOM when keys are duplicated

description: component.mbt - component_slots, component_counter, trigger_render etc. are module-level globals. Two start() calls corrupt each other. Known limitation - document or address.
notes:
Referenced again by the March 12, 2026 code review.

This corresponds to the finding that component/app runtime state is global to the package, so multiple start() calls can interfere with each other through shared component_slots, component_counter, and trigger_render.
title: Arch: global mutable state prevents multiple app instances

close_reason: Fixed: component dispatch now uses while loop to re-check queue after clear(), matching app.mbt pattern. Prevents message loss from synchronous cmd dispatches.
description: Both app.mbt and component.mbt have near-identical push->is_updating guard->for loop->update->cmd.run->clear patterns. The component version also has a correctness risk: after clear(), it doesn't re-check the queue, so messages dispatched by cmd.run during the last batch could be dropped. Extract a shared helper.
title: Dedup: unify message queue drain pattern between app.mbt and component.mbt

close_reason: Unmount now invalidates component handles with one-time warning dispatch
description:
When a component slot is removed, `Handle.dispatch` is not invalidated. Parents that keep a handle can still `Cmd::send` into an unmounted child loop, retaining model/queue closures and causing ghost updates + memory retention.

Fix direction: track handles per slot and set them to no-op when the slot is removed, or gate dispatch by slot liveness.
title: Unmounted component handles retain live dispatch closures

description:
Review findings from the March 12, 2026 codebase review.

Scope:
- runtime correctness bugs in app mounting and component reuse
- API correctness gaps in event helpers and subscription reconciliation
- architectural cleanup around global state and duplicated Attr handling

This epic groups the concrete follow-up issues created from that review.
title: Code review round 4: runtime correctness and API design

description:
app.mbt currently appends the initial vnode to the mount element, but later renders diff the mount parent at child index 0.

If the selector matches a non-empty container, updates patch the wrong DOM node instead of the node Chai mounted.

Affected code:
- app.mbt:36
- app.mbt:72-73

Expected direction:
- track the actual mounted root node, or clear/own the mount container before first render
- add a regression test that mounts into a non-empty container and verifies the second render updates the Chai-owned node
title: Fix start() diffing against pre-existing mount children

description:
component.mbt reuses existing component slots by calling the stored render closure only. The stored render/update/handle wiring is never refreshed from the latest parent render.

That means a component with the same id keeps stale captured parent data forever, and replacing the Handle for the same id has no effect.

Affected code:
- component.mbt:75-79
- component.mbt:90-117

Expected direction:
- update reused slots with the latest view/update/handle closures before rendering
- add regression tests for changed captured parent data and swapping handles for the same component id
title: Refresh component closures and handle wiring on slot reuse

description:
attr.mbt implements on_input() and on_change() by casting event.target() to HTMLInputElement.

The README presents these as general string-value helpers, but the current implementation breaks for textarea and select.

Affected code:
- attr.mbt:29-40
- attr.mbt:44-50
- README.md:112-120

Expected direction:
- decode value from the appropriate form element abstraction instead of assuming HTMLInputElement
- add tests covering input, textarea, and select usage
- align docs with the supported surface if the helper intentionally stays narrower
title: Generalize on_input/on_change beyond HTMLInputElement

description:
app.mbt keeps subscriptions alive solely by key. If a subscription is returned with the same key but a different closure or configuration, the old subscription stays mounted and the new behavior is ignored.

This can leave stale closures in custom subscriptions and in helpers like Sub::every or Sub::on_key_down with a changed prevent_default predicate.

Affected code:
- app.mbt:82-110
- README.md:159-169

Expected direction:
- decide and document the subscription identity model explicitly
- either restart subscriptions when behavior/config changes, or make the same-key contract much more explicit in the API and docs
- add regression tests for same-key subscriptions whose behavior changes across renders
title: Reconcile subscriptions by behavior changes, not key only

description:
Attr handling is duplicated across VNode event binding, initial DOM creation, and attribute diffing.

Adding or changing an Attr variant currently requires synchronized edits in multiple match statements, which raises the odds of drift and partial support.

Affected code:
- vnode.mbt:35-60
- diff.mbt:61-85
- diff.mbt:357-520

Expected direction:
- extract a single internal abstraction for Attr classification/application
- keep event wrapping, initial application, and diff behavior consistent through that abstraction
- simplify the match-heavy imperative code where possible without regressing the hot path
title: Deduplicate Attr application and diff logic

description:
Backfill a regression test for the mount-container bug tracked in chai-47u.1.

Coverage goal:
- mount Chai into a selector whose container already has child nodes
- trigger a second render
- verify the update patches the Chai-owned node rather than an unrelated pre-existing child

This should fail on the current implementation and pass once the mount ownership bug is fixed.
title: Add regression test for mounting into a non-empty root container

description:
Add explicit regression coverage for the stale component-slot reuse behavior tracked in chai-47u.2.

Coverage goal:
- re-render a component with the same id but different captured parent data and verify the child sees the latest data
- swap the Handle passed to the same component id and verify Cmd::send targets the latest handle wiring

These cases are currently untested and should become stable unit or browser regressions before the runtime is changed.
title: Backfill component slot reuse regression tests

description:
Backfill API-compatibility tests for the event-helper gap tracked in chai-47u.3.

Coverage goal:
- exercise on_input and on_change on input, textarea, and select elements
- verify String extraction works for each supported form element
- catch regressions if docs and implementation drift again

Prefer a fast unit-level test if the webapi test harness can model these targets; otherwise use a small browser regression.
title: Add textarea/select coverage for on_input and on_change

description:
Backfill tests for the subscription identity gap tracked in chai-47u.4.

Coverage goal:
- return a subscription with the same key but changed behavior/configuration across renders
- verify whether Chai intentionally preserves or refreshes the subscription, and lock that decision down with tests
- cover at least one built-in helper case and one custom Sub::sub case if possible

This should turn the current ambiguity into an explicit, test-backed contract.
title: Add regression coverage for same-key subscription changes

close_reason: Extracted a_with_attrs helper shared by link() and hash_link().
description: nav.mbt:137-172 - Both functions create prefix attrs array, push user attrs, then call a(). Extract a shared helper.
title: Dedup: link and hash_link duplicate prefix-attrs-then-call-a() pattern

close_reason: Updated README on_key_down docs to show predicate-based prevent_default usage
description:
README says `prevent_default=true` for `Sub::on_key_down`, but API expects a predicate `(String) -> Bool`. This mismatch can mislead users and cause compile-time friction.

Fix direction: update docs example and explanation to show predicate usage.
title: README on_key_down prevent_default docs mismatch runtime signature

close_reason: Replaced manual Some/None match with handler(e).map(f) in Attr::map for EventMaybe.
description: attr.mbt:17-21 - Manual match on Some/None should be replaced with handler(e).map(f).
title: Simplify: Attr::map EventMaybe uses manual Some/None match

close_reason: Inlined remove_unless into remove_unused_component_slots, its only call site.
description: component.mbt - remove_unless is a generic helper used exactly once in remove_unused_component_slots. Inline it.
title: Simplify: inline remove_unless into its single call site

close_reason: Added fast-path pairwise comparison in diff_attrs for same-length attr lists. Avoids Map/HashSet allocation in the common case.
description: diff.mbt - Allocates Map, two HashSets, and another Map on every diff pass for every element, even with 1-3 attrs. Add fast-path pairwise comparison for same-length attr lists to avoid allocation in common case.
title: Perf: diff_attrs allocates Map + 2 HashSets unconditionally

close_reason: Removed 3 redundant length > 0 guards before clear() in app.mbt and component.mbt.
description: app.mbt, component.mbt (3 sites) - clear() on empty array is a no-op. Remove the if-length-check guards.
title: Simplify: remove redundant length > 0 guards before clear()

close_reason: link() now intercepts only plain primary same-tab clicks via conditional event dispatch
description:
`link()` always calls `prevent_default()` on click and dispatches `on_nav`. This blocks ctrl/cmd-click, shift-click, middle-click, and other native navigation semantics.

Fix direction: intercept only primary unmodified clicks targeting same browsing context; otherwise allow native browser behavior.
title: SPA link helper breaks modified-click/new-tab browser behavior

close_reason: All 12 child issues resolved. 10 fixed, 1 was false positive (error_message bug), 1 won't fix (singleton Cmd::none). Global state issue (chai-3vy) remains open as P4 backlog.
description: Comprehensive code review findings from round 3.
title: Code review round 3: bugs, perf, simplification, dedup

close_reason: Hoisted HTMLElement cast into lazy get_html_el() helper in diff_attrs, avoiding repeated casts per style attr.
description: diff.mbt - The cast to_js(el).into() to HTMLElement is done once per Style in both apply_attrs and diff_attrs loops. Hoist to a single local on first style encounter.
title: Perf: HTMLElement cast repeated per style attr in loops

close_reason: error_message logic was actually correct; original null check was the right guard. Reviewed and confirmed.
description: cmd.mbt:71 - When reason is null/undefined, it calls reason.to_string() (returns 'null'). When it's NOT null, it tries to extract .message. The branches are swapped. Also doesn't check is_undefined().
title: Bug: error_message has inverted null-check logic

close_reason: Changed old_key_map from Map to @hashmap.HashMap for O(1) lookups in keyed diff.
description: diff.mbt - old_key_map is Map[String, ...] with O(log n) lookups. Should use HashMap for O(1). seen_new_keys already uses HashSet.
title: Perf: old_key_map uses tree Map instead of HashMap

close_reason: Cannot cache singleton due to MoonBit generic type system - Array types don't unify across type params. Would need unsafe %identity cast for no meaningful gain. Won't fix.
description: cmd.mbt:8, sub.mbt:14 - These are called constantly in every update cycle. Cache a singleton empty Cmd/Sub instead of allocating new arrays.
title: Perf: Cmd::none() and Sub::none() allocate fresh array every call

close_reason: Added vnode tag helper generator script and generated wrapper block markers
description:
`vnode.mbt` contains many near-identical wrappers (`div`, `span`, `h1`, `h2`, ...), each delegating to `el`. This is easy to drift and increases maintenance overhead.

Fix direction: generate wrappers from a tag list (script or macro) and keep a single source of truth for supported elements.
title: Consolidate repetitive VNode tag constructors with generation

close_reason: Replaced queue copy/clear loops with index-based draining in app/component runtime
description:
`app.boot`, component dispatch loops, and pending init command draining repeatedly call `copy()` + `clear()` on arrays each cycle. Under bursty message loads this adds avoidable allocations and throughput overhead.

Fix direction: use double-buffer queue swapping or index-based draining to preserve reentrancy without cloning full arrays each cycle.
title: Reduce queue copy churn in runtime dispatch loops

close_reason: Implemented keyed/non-keyed mode-switch fallback replacement in diff_children
description:
`diff_children` switches to keyed reconciliation based only on the new children list. When old children are unkeyed and new children are keyed, `diff_children_keyed` builds an empty old key map and inserts new nodes without removing old DOM nodes, leaving stale siblings in the DOM.

Repro shape: old children `[A, B]`, new children `[keyed("k", C)]` => DOM becomes `[C, A, B]`.

Fix direction: detect old keyed mode too; if mode changes across renders, either hard-fail with a clear abort or fall back to full replacement of the child list.
title: Keyed diff leaves stale DOM when switching from unkeyed to keyed children
